pages.title: Employers | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Career Center offers organizations and businesses recruiting services, programs, and events to connect alumni with employment opportunities.
contentBlocks: The Career Center is the central hub for career and employment services at Sacramento State. Working with businesses and organizations of all sizes and from all sectors, we are committed to delivering the very best value for your recruiting efforts.
Finding great Sacramento State students is easy with our free online career services management system, Handshake.
Schedule recruitment events such as career fairs, company presentations (information sessions), on-campus interviews, and table events

Hornet Partners receive exclusive benefits and custom services designed to enhance brand awareness and support hiring goals. The generous support of our Hornet Partners is essential to our ability to provide the robust career services available to all Sac State students and alumni.
Convenience: One payment can cover recruiting expenses for a year
Branding: Benefit from enhanced visibility and targeted marketing
Strategic engagement: Work closely with our team to connect with Sac State talent
Start building your campus network today! Contact
Phone: (916) 278-6231 careercenter@csus.edu
Follow us on https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCZDFrk6TWpcb-Dm1lfcgoJw?view_as=subscriber

links.text: Learn About Our Hornet Partner Program

pages.title: Student Engagement Opportunities | Sacramento State
pages.description: On this page you will find information for employers about student engagement opportunities.
headings: Spring 1-Day Education, Youth, and Family Services Career Fair
contentBlocks: Open to all majors, this event is an opportunity for recruiters to meet with students and alumni from all colleges to share information about job and internship opportunities and careers within their organizations.

This event is an opportunity for recruiters to meet students and alumni aspiring to enter the field of education, who are passionate about working with youth, and/or possess a desire to fortify communities by providing supportive services for families. This fair historically attracts students and alumni from teacher credential, social work, counseling, nursing, recreation, child development, family studies, and social science programs. The fair also reaches liberal arts, STEM, and business majors interested in meaningful internship, summer, part-time, full-time, fellowship or service corps opportunities with education and social assistance organizations.

In order to attend the Career Fairs, your organization must sign a Release of Liability Waiver (available at employer check-in table). To expedite the registration process, you can email careerfair@csus.edu with a signed waiver in advance. Please note a wet signature is required.

Within 2-3 weeks of the Career Fair date, an Employer Packet with information regarding parking, shipping materials, etc. will be sent out via email. If you are registering for the Career Fair and not attending, please ensure that the Employer Packet is shared with all representatives who will be attending.
If you would like to be notified when employer registration is open for any of our career fairs, complete our Employer Contact Form and choose “Career Fairs” under Activity Interest.
Employer Contact Form Supporting Students with Reasonable Accommodations:

The Career Center offers early access to all our career fairs for students who require reasonable accommodations and may find crowded environments challenging, to ensure inclusivity and equal opportunities for all participants. As part of our mission to provide equitable services, we highly encourage employers to be set up one hour early to meet these accommodations.

Hornet Partners are invited to lead workshops on career/professional development topics (e.g., resume development, interview techniques, LinkedIn 101, etc.) based on students’ needs. These workshops offer an excellent opportunity to share your organization’s expertise, connect with students and enhance your brand visibility. These workshops take place on Wednesdays at varying times. Workshops can be held virtually or in-person (pending space availability). If you are interested in becoming a Hornet Partner, please email us at careercenter@csus.edu .

Please note: We begin booking for each semester during the previous semester (e.g., We begin booking for Spring 2025 during Fall 2024). Spaces fill up quickly and our Hornet Partners receive priority scheduling.

Hornet Partners can table at Lassen Hall (where the Career Center is located) to engage with students. We will provide tables, chairs and a canopy if the weather requires it. Tabling takes place on Tuesdays, Wednesdays and Thursdays. Hornet Partners can table varying amounts depending on their level of partnership. If you are interested in becoming a Hornet Partner, please email us at careercenter@csus.edu .
Non-Hornet partners are encouraged to connect directly with the appropriate academic department or college aligned with their recruitment interests to explore tabling opportunities.
If you prefer to table specifically in coordination with a student organization, please contact Student Organizations & Leadership (SO&L) at hornet.marketplace@csus.edu

If you are interested in exploring a request to rent facilities for a third party tabling session linked to the university's mission, and allowable per commercial solicitation and current policy, please be advised the process can take 60+ days. Please access Facilities Reservations for Community

Our Hornet Partner Program offers a year-long, customized recruitment experience that includes career fair fees, premium booth placement, social media promotion, and targeted student engagement. For more details, visit our Hornet Partner Program. You can also view a list of current Hornet Partners on our Jobs Page .

Please note: The program is currently full. If you’re interested in becoming a Hornet Partner, check back in Summer 2026 for potential openings for the 2026–2027 academic year. For other partnership opportunities, please contact Abraham Mendoza.
If you are interested in presenting within classrooms, we highly encourage you to reach out to that individual department/college directly.

If you are interested in learning more about promoting your internships or increasing your current internship opportunities. We encourage you to use the following resources on our Internships website .

pages.title: Help with Handshake | Sacramento State
pages.description: Get setup with Handshake; a place where students are connected to employers looking to hire for part-time, full-time, and seasonal jobs and internships. Create your account, connect to Sac State, post a job, and register for career fairs!
contentBlocks: Have an account already and want to connect with California State University, Sacramento?
If you are already connected in Handshake to California State University, Sacramento, here are some great tutorials to get you started.
Quick Tip: It's a great idea to add Handshake to your list of safe email senders.
Correspondence will be sent to you from handshake@m.joinhandshake.com or handshake@notifications.joinhandshake.com .
Here is an article about how to ensure the deliverability of Handshake emails .
For more assistance, please contact handshake@csus.edu .
For posting FWS jobs on Handshake, please note the following:
Do NOT use the work study designation on Handshake for your job post.
Include FWS in the job title of the job posting so it can be easily identified as a work study opportunity by applicants.

pages.title: 2026 Career Fair Employer Guide | Sacramento State
pages.description: This page provides information and resources for employers part of the Hornet Partner Program with the Career Center at Sacramento State.
headings: Directions to Campus and Designated Parking Structure
Directions to Loading Dock (from College Town Drive):
Directions to Loading Dock (from J Street):
Employer Relations, Volunteer Coordinator, & Handshake Assistance
Employer Relations, Hornet Partner Program, & Handshake Assistance
contentBlocks: Thank you for being a part of Sacramento State’s Spring 2026 Job & Internship Career Fair AND/OR Education, Youth and Family Services Career Fair. We are excited to collaborate with you to meet your recruitment needs and we are looking forward to working with you to make the event a great success.

Check-in time is 8 -10 a.m. We recommend arriving early to allow sufficient time to get from Parking Structure 3 to the University Union, drop off materials, and set up. The Career Center is offering early access between 10 - 10:30 a.m. for students who require reasonable accommodations and may find crowded environments challenging, and to ensure inclusivity and equal opportunities for all participants. As part of our mission to provide equitable services, we highly encourage employers to arrive early and complete setup by 10 a.m. To request reasonable ADA accommodations, please contact us at least five days prior to the event at careercenter@csus.edu or (916) 278-6231.
Please share the link to this page with all representatives who will be staffing your booth, for it covers the following pertinent event details:
Keep to the right and take the loop and follow under highway
Turn left at White Poplar Way (1st stop sign) to park in Parking Structure 3 OR turn right onto Callery Pear Way to enter Parking Structure 3
Turn left at White Poplar Way (1st stop sign) to park in Parking structure 3 OR turn right onto Callery Pear Way to enter Parking Structure 3
Follow State University Drive past the bus stop
Turn right at White Poplar Way to park in Parking Structure 3 OR turn right onto Callery Pear Way to enter Parking Structure 3

If needed, employers can unload materials at the loading dock in the back of the University Union (Pink Marker) following Atlas Cedar way. Staff will be available to assist you with your materials. It is highly recommended that you arrive early to unload and park in student parking spaces on floors 4, 5, or 6 (excluding carpool spaces) in Parking Structure 3 (highlighted in Yellow with Green Marker) OR for ADA requirements park in Lot 5 behind the University Union (Red Marker) and walk to the University Union entrance.
Turn right onto State University Drive, go straight then turn left at Atlas Cedar Way, continue past Parking Structure 2.
Turn onto State University Drive and follow it towards the left, continue on State University Drive.
Turn right at Atlas Cedar Way and continue past Parking Structure 2 (Purple Marker).

Parking is included in your registration fee. Floors 4, 5, and 6 of Parking Structure 3 are reserved for Career Fair participants (excluding carpool spaces). ADA parking is located in Lot 5 (adjacent to University Union). Please note, you could be subject to a fine if you park outside of the designated areas.

Career Fair staff and volunteers will be stationed at Parking Structure 3, and will direct participants to the University Union and can provide help, if needed. Please notify the Career Center at careercenter@csus.edu or (916) 278-6231 five (5) days prior to the event if ADA accommodations are needed.

Sacramento State and all of its campus entities accept no responsibility for damages or parking tickets that occur when on campus. We highly encourage you to park in the designated areas listed above. If you have an oversized vehicle, please reach out to us at least five (5) days in advance so we can make accommodations.
Employer check-in will take place from 8 - 10 a.m. at the Career Center table inside the University Union Ballroom.

The Career Center table will be open all day to address questions or concerns. The Career Center is offering early access between 10 - 10:30 a.m. for students who require reasonable accommodations and may find crowded environments challenging, and to ensure inclusivity and equal opportunities for all participants. As part of our mission to provide equitable services, we highly encourage employers to arrive early and complete setup by 10 a.m.

Your organization is assigned a table based on your requests for electricity, large displays, and number of representatives. We have done our best to accommodate all requests and CANNOT accommodate specific table locations or table sizes on the day of the event. PLEASE NOTE: We strictly enforce the number of representatives per registration. Due to space restrictions, each organization is allowed 2 representatives, and if paid for in advance, up to 5 representatives maximum. Payment can no longer be accepted at the Career Fair. Prepayment is required.

Upon checking in, employers will receive a booth map, daily schedule, and evaluation QR code. If additional items are needed, such as tape, markers, clips, etc., please check to see if the Career Center table has the supplies you need. Per new campus sustainability policies, we are unable to provide plastic bottled water. Water stations will be available at the Career Center Table and in the Cottonwood Suite, 2nd floor. In addition, there are several water fountains located within the University Union; therefore, we recommend your representatives bring their own refillable water bottles.

The Career Fair ends at 2 p.m. Students arrive at various times throughout the career fair; therefore, we encourage employers not to checkout before 2 p.m. Staff will be available to assist with taking your materials back to the loading dock if needed. Please request assistance at the Career Center Table. PLEASE NOTE: Instructions for shipping back displays and materials from the event have changed. Please visit the Shipping Instructions section for more information.
We value your partnership and feedback and encourage you to complete our Career Fair evaluation.
An evaluation QR code will be provided on the day of the fair.

LIABILITY WAIVER. In order to attend the Career Fair, each participating representative from your organization must sign a Release of Liability Waiver (available at employer check-in table). To expedite the check-in process, you can email careerfair@csus.edu to obtain waiver in advance.
PLEASE NOTE: A wet signature is required.

COVID-19 . Sacramento State recommends that all students, staff, faculty, and visitors be vaccinated for COVID-19. Large events pose a risk to those who are not vaccinated. While masks are no longer required indoors, masks and hand sanitizer will be made available to those who want it at the event. Visit the following site for the latest recommendations for Sacramento County.

RECRUITMENT PRACTICES. All employers who conduct recruiting activities utilizing any Career Center resources are required to comply with all Federal Equal Employment Opportunity EEO Laws , California State Labor Standards Enforcement (DLSE) Law , and the National Association of Colleges and Employers (NACE) Principles for Professional Practice . The Career Center reserves the right to exercise its broad discretion and refuse service to anyone. All job and internship listings are posted at the discretion of the Career Center, and we reserve the right to choose not to post a position if it does not appear to support the best interests of the students and/or Sacramento State.

Several campus policies related to shipping have changed which require additional steps for staff and employers. Materials and displays for the Career Fair must be shipped to Central Receiving, delivered to the Career Center for storage, and then transported by Career Center Staff and volunteers to the University Union.
Please note: Retain your shipping tracking number(s), label packages clearly, and ship only what’s necessary.
Sacramento State and all of its campus entities accept no responsibility for lost or stolen materials and displays all items shipped are at the sender’s risk.

You can begin shipping your materials at any time, but you should plan for them to arrive on or before Wednesday, February 25th to allow for sorting and transport. NOTE: If materials are not received by this date, we cannot guarantee that your materials will be present at the Career Fair.
PLEASE INFORM THE CAREER CENTER IF YOU PLAN TO SEND ANY MATERIALS
Vicky Tan / Career Fair Sacramento State Career Center, MS 6064 6000 J Street Sacramento, CA 95819
Vendors can take ALL USPS AND UPS materials to the HORNET BOOKSTORE to the cashier desk. All packages must be pre-labeled with a tracking number ready to be shipped out.
The Hornet Bookstore is not able to print any UPS labels.
FedEx materials will need to be taken to a FedEx location by the vendor. The Hornet Bookstore does not manage FedEx shipping

Interested in a year-long, customized recruitment experience, that includes career fair fees, premium booth location, social media outreach, and targeted student engagement? Our Hornet Partner Program offers a year-long, customized recruitment experience that includes career fair fees, premium booth placement, social media promotion, and targeted student engagement.

At this time, the program is full. If you’re interested in becoming a Hornet Partner, please check back in Summer 2026 for potential openings for the 2026–2027 academic year. For alternative partnership opportunities please contact Abraham Mendoza.
Mark your calendars for the Fall 2026 Job & Internship Career Fair scheduled for October 5th and 6th.
Sacramento State Career Center Lassen Hall 1013 6000 J Street Sacramento, CA 95819-6064 P: (916) 278-6231 E: careercenter@csus.edu
Melissa Repa, Ed.D. Director E: melissa.repa@csus.edu
Abraham Mendoza, Lead Employer Relations/Internship & Professional Programs Coordinator E: abraham.mendoza@csus.edu
Angelica Villalobos, Employer Relations Specialist E: angelica.villalobos@csus.edu

Ana Uribe, Administrative Support Coordinator E: ana.uribe@csus.edu
